heading: Treatment of primary caretaker parents and other individuals

children.text: the term “correctional officer” means a correctional officer of the Bureau of Prisons;
the term “covered institution” means a Federal penal or correctional institution;
the term “Director” means the Director of the Bureau of Prisons;
the term “post-partum recovery” means the first 12-week period of post-partum recovery after giving birth;
the term “primary caretaker parent” has the meaning given the term in section 31903 of the Family Unity Demonstration Project Act ();34 U.S.C. 12242
the term “prisoner” means an individual who is incarcerated in a Federal penal or correctional institution, including a vulnerable person; and

children.label: A
children.text: is under 21 years of age or over 60 years of age;

children.label: B
children.text: is pregnant;

children.label: C
children.text: is victim or witness of a crime;

children.label: D
children.text: has filed a nonfrivolous civil rights claim in Federal or State court; or

children.label: i
children.text: by any court or administrative judicial proceeding;

children.label: ii
children.text: by any corrections official;

children.label: iii
children.text: by the individual’s attorney or legal service provider; or

children.label: iv
children.text: by the individual.

children.heading: .—Establishment of office
children.label: 1 .—Establishment of office
children.text: The Director shall establish within the Bureau of Prisons an office that determines the placement of prisoners.

children.label: A
children.text: if the prisoner has children, consider placing the prisoner as close to the children as possible; and

children.label: B
children.text: consider any other factor that the office determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Prohibition on Placement of Pregnant Prisoners or Prisoners in Post-partum Recovery in Segregated Housing Units.—
children.label: c Prohibition on Placement of Pregnant Prisoners or Prisoners in Post-partum Recovery in Segregated Housing Units.—

children.heading: .—Placement in segregated housing units
children.label: 1 .—Placement in segregated housing units
children.text: A covered institution may not place a prisoner who is pregnant or in post-partum recovery in a segregated housing unit unless the prisoner presents an immediate risk of harm to the prisoner or others.

children.heading: .—Restrictions
children.label: 2 .—Restrictions
children.text: Any placement of a prisoner described in paragraph (1) in a segregated housing unit shall be limited and temporary.

children.heading: .—Intake and Assessments
children.label: d .—Intake and Assessments
children.text: The Director shall assess the need for family-focused programming at intake, such as questions about children, gauge interest in parenting resources, and concerns about their child or caregiving, and administer ongoing assessment to better inform, identify, and make recommendations about the mother’s parental role and familial needs.

children.heading: .—Parenting Classes
children.label: e .—Parenting Classes
children.text: The Director shall provide voluntary parenting classes to each prisoner who is a primary caretaker parent, and such classes shall be made available to prisoners with limited English proficiency in compliance with title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 ( et seq.).42 U.S.C. 2000d
identify a prisoner who may have a mental or physical health need relating to trauma the prisoner has experienced; and
refer a prisoner described in paragraph (1) to the proper health care professional for diagnosis and treatment.
how to interact with children in an age-appropriate manner, and the children’s caregivers;
basic childhood and adolescent development information; and
basic customer service skills.

children.heading: .—Health care access
children.label: 1 .—Health care access
children.text: The Director shall ensure that all prisoners receive adequate health care.

children.label: A
children.text: on the distribution and accessibility of sanitary products to prisoners, to ensure each prisoner who requires these products receives a quantity the prisoner deems sufficient; and

children.label: B
children.text: providing that no visitor is prohibited from visiting a prisoner due to the visitor’s use of sanitary products.

children.heading: .—Gynecologist access
children.label: 3 .—Gynecologist access
children.text: The Director shall ensure that all prisoners have access to a gynecologist as appropriate.

children.heading: .—Relation to other laws
children.label: 4 .—Relation to other laws
children.text: Nothing in paragraph (1) shall be construed to affect the requirements under the Prison Rape Elimination Act of 2003 ( et seq.).34 U.S.C. 30301
